key_insight: The paper shows that, under standard transport stability assumptions, learning any valid transport map is statistically as hard as learning the optimal transport map, but if those assumptions fail, non-OT maps can be much easier to estimate.
engineering_relevance: This gives a minimax lower-bound framework for judging the sample efficiency of diffusion models, flow matching, and other transport-based generative methods when their learned outputs are evaluated as maps or plans.
method: Formulates valid transport-map estimation as a rigorous minimax statistical problem.
Derives sample-complexity lower bounds that apply broadly to transport-based generative methods.
Uses examples to contrast the stable regime, where OT-like difficulty persists, with unstable settings where alternative maps are easier to learn.
evidence_signal: The evidence is theoretical rather than empirical: the abstract describes minimax lower-bound analysis and illustrative examples, not a dataset-driven evaluation.
watch_item: The strongest hardness result depends on standard but strong stability assumptions from OT theory, so the conclusions may not transfer to all real-world generative settings.
paper_title: The Fundamental Limits of Valid Transport Map Estimation
